title: Arrays, Pointers, Arithmetic, and Bounds · Object-Lifetime Lens: Slice Pointer Length
summary: A raster pipeline slices rows and subviews while changed dimensions expose one-past, negative-offset, and lost-bound defects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem slice pointer length increment in bounded image-slice library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent a borrowed slice with pointer, length, mutability, lifetime, and aliasing contracts that travel together.
State and check lower and upper index invariants before access, including zero-length and changed-shape cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Slice Pointer Length · Theory Slice Pointer Length
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1151. Produce bounded C evidence to validate a half-open array slice before access; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
